summary: px8my ships a browser redirector that hijacks pages loading it via CDN
details: The package's main file px.js is browser-side JavaScript that injects a fullscreen iframe pointing at a hardcoded remote URL (https://mfmz.gzhrwq.cn/H.html?c=0gjr), covering the host page with a loading mask and hijacking the viewport of any site that loads the script (e.g., via jsDelivr's automatic npm mirror at cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/px8my/px.js). The shipped update_px8my.sh script accepts a new domain, rewrites the redirect URL in px.js, bumps the patch version, runs npm publish, and issues a jsDelivr purge against /npm/px8my/px.js — tooling that indicates the maintainer operates the package as a rotating browser-redirection service using npm+jsDelivr as delivery infrastructure. Any web application that pulls px8my from a CDN will have its viewport taken over and its visitors redirected to a domain the maintainer swaps at will.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
